title: capparis spinosa subsp. rupestris
intro: Capparis spinosa subsp. rupestris, also known as rock caper, is a subspecies of the caper plant, a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The rock caper has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and providing antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and salves, and is also used as a food ingredient in some traditional cuisines.
